lesson: Two-phase extraction (parse then resolve) is essential for cross-file references. The first phase records unresolved references as data; the second phase, after all files are seen, promotes them to edges.

In a multi-agent system without peer messaging, 'memory-as-bus + lead-orchestrated phases' is the only reliable coordination pattern. The lead reads outputs from a shared store and spawns the next agent with explicit input keys.
origin: hard-won across multiple prior agent system designs

lesson: For a code graph queried by AI agents 90% of the time, the hot path is single-symbol lookup and 1-3 hop traversal. Optimize trunk lookup and synapse adjacency for these cases; do not over-engineer deep graph algorithms.
origin: benchmark analysis of codegraph and Neo4j

lesson: AI consumers benefit massively (3-4x) from compact symbolic serialization (Emmet-style) vs JSON. Token efficiency is a first-class design constraint, not a polish item.
origin: empirical observation across AI coding workflows

lesson: When the eventual optimal data structure (radix trie) is significantly more complex than a correct-but-suboptimal one (HashMap), ship the simple version with the full test suite first. Tests pin semantics; the data-structure swap is then a pure refactor with bench deltas — the cleanest possible PR.

A materialized-path scheme MUST distinguish 'startsWith(prefix+separator)' from 'startsWith(prefix)' when computing descendants — otherwise `Auth` will incorrectly match `AuthService`. Regression test: `trunk_descendants_must_not_match_prefix_of_sibling_segment_name`.
